summary: Ukrainian forces are intensifying the use of FPV drones to strike deep behind Russian lines, with reports of attacks on logistics near Donetsk and a "carousel" of seven drones targeting Peski, about 25-30 km from the front. Russian operators from the "Rubikon" Center claim to have destroyed 162 Ukrainian UAV control and deployment points, including fixed-wing UAVs, across multiple directions on March 10. The "Molniya" strike drone is increasingly appearing on the front, with its control frequencies remaining difficult for Russian electronic warfare to counter. Meanwhile, the flow of Shahed-136/131 drones to Ukraine has sharply decreased, likely due to production issues, enhanced Russian EW and interceptor drone activity, and the loss of Starlink connectivity. A Russian soldier was hit by two Ukrainian FPV drones simultaneously, and a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed by a Russian fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" in Dobropillia.
